heading: Notification of grade crossing problems
label: 20152 Notification of grade crossing problems
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of the Rail Safety Improvement Act of 2008, referred to in subsec. (a), is the date of enactment of div. A of , which was approved .Pub. L. 110–432Oct. 16, 2008

Amendments2008— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, section related to a pilot program to demonstrate a system to provide emergency notification of grade crossing problems.Pub. L. 110–4321996—Subsec. (b). substituted “” for “the date of enactment of this section” and “, an evaluation” for “that date an evaluation”.Pub. L. 104–287November 2, 1994November 2, 1994

Prior to amendment, section related to a pilot program to demonstrate a system to provide emergency notification of grade crossing problems.Pub. L. 110–4321996—Subsec. (b). substituted “” for “the date of enactment of this section” and “, an evaluation” for “that date an evaluation”.Pub. L. 104–287November 2, 1994November 2, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: malfunctions of signals, crossing gates, and other devices to promote safety at the grade crossing of railroad tracks on those rights-of-way and public or private roads;

children.label: B
children.text: disabled vehicles blocking railroad tracks at such grade crossings;

children.label: C
children.text: obstructions to the view of a pedestrian or a vehicle operator for a reasonable distance in either direction of a train’s approach; or

children.label: D
children.text: other safety information involving such grade crossings;
upon receiving a report pursuant to paragraph (1)(A) or (B), immediately contact trains operating near the grade crossing to warn them of the malfunction or disabled vehicle;

upon receiving a report pursuant to paragraph (1)(A) or (B), and after contacting trains pursuant to paragraph (2), contact, as necessary, appropriate public safety officials having jurisdiction over the grade crossing to provide them with the information necessary for them to direct traffic, assist in the removal of the disabled vehicle, or carry out other activities as appropriate;
upon receiving a report pursuant to paragraph (1)(C) or (D), timely investigate the report, remove the obstruction if possible, or correct the unsafe circumstance; and

children.label: A
children.text: a toll-free telephone number to be used for placing calls described in paragraph (1) to the railroad carrier dispatching trains on that right-of-way;

children.label: B
children.text: an explanation of the purpose of that toll-free telephone number; and

children.label: C
children.text: the grade crossing number assigned for that crossing by the National Highway-Rail Crossing Inventory established by the Department of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Waiver
children.label: b .—Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirement that the telephone service be toll-free for Class II and Class III rail carriers if the Secretary determines that toll-free service would be cost prohibitive or unnecessary.
